question: There are two examination rooms A and B. If 10 students are sent from A to B, then the number of students in each room is the same. If 20 candidates are sent from B to A, then the number of students in A is double the number of students in B. The number of students in room A is:

The price of commodity X increases by 40 paise every year, while the price of commodity Y increases by 15 paise every year. If in 2001, the price of commodity X was Rs. 4.20 and that of Y was Rs. 6.30, in which year will commodity X cost 40 paise more than commodity Y?
If a - b = 3 and a² + b² = 29, find the value of ab.
The salaries A, B, and C are in the ratio 2:3:5. If increments of 15%, 10%, and 20% are allowed in their salaries, then what will be the new ratio of their salaries?

A and B entered into a partnership with capitals in the ratio 4:5. After 3 months, A withdrew some capital and B withdrew some of his capital. The gain at the end of 10 months was Rs. 760. A's share in the profit is:

What is R's share of profit in a joint venture? I. Q started business investing Rs. 80,000. II. R joined him after 3 months. III. P joined after 4 months with a capital of Rs. 1,20,000 and got Rs. 6000 as his share profit.
option_d: Even with all I, II, and III, the answer cannot be arrived at

question: A alone can do a piece of work in 6 days and B alone in 8 days. A and B undertook to do it for Rs. 3200. With the help of C, they completed the work in 3 days. How much is to be paid to C?

What is the volume of a cube? I. The area of each face of the cube is 64 square metres. II. The length of one side of the cube is 8 metres.
option_a: I alone sufficient while II alone not sufficient
option_b: II alone sufficient while I alone not sufficient
option_c: Either I or II alone sufficient to answer
option_d: Both I and II are not sufficient

Three candidates contested an election and received 1136, 7636, and 11628 votes, respectively. What percentage of the total votes did the winning candidate get?

In how many days can 10 women finish a work? I. 10 men can complete the work in 6 days. II. 10 men and 10 women together can complete the work in 3 3/7 days. III. If 10 men work for 3 days and then 10 women replace them, the remaining work is completed in 4 days.

8 men and 14 women are working together in a field. After 3 days, 5 men and 8 women leave. How many more days will be required to complete the work? I. 19 men and 12 women together can complete the work in 18 days. II. 16 men can complete two-thirds of the work in 16 days. III. In 1 day, the work done by three men is equal to the work done by four women.
A towel, when bleached, was found to have lost 20% of its length and 10% of its breadth. The percentage decrease in area is:
What is the capacity of a cylindrical tank? I. Radius of the base is half of its height which is 28 metres. II. Area of the base is 616 sq. metres and its height is 28 metres.
option_a: I alone sufficient while II alone not sufficient
option_b: II alone sufficient while I alone not sufficient
option_c: Either I or II alone sufficient to answer
option_d: Both I and II are not sufficient

option_a: To define a contract for classes to implement
option_b: To define a class that cannot be instantiated
option_c: To define a class that can be extended by other classes
option_d: To define a class that can be used as a template for creating objects

question: What is the purpose of the yield statement in Python?
option_a: To exit a function and return a value
option_b: To pause the execution of a function and return a value
option_c: To raise an exception in a function

question: What is the purpose of the lambda keyword in Python?
option_a: To define a function with a single expression
option_b: To define a function with multiple expressions
option_c: To define a function with a name
option_d: To define a function with a return statement

question: What is the purpose of the try-except block in Python?
option_a: To handle exceptions that may occur during the execution of a program
option_b: To define a function that may raise an exception
option_c: To define a module that may raise an exception
option_d: To define a class that may raise an exception

question: What is the purpose of the super() function in Python
option_a: To call a method in a parent class from a child class
option_b: To create a new instance of a class

question: Which of the following is not a programming language?
Which of the following is not a data structure?
What is the time complexity of the binary search algorithm?
Which of the following is not a type of software testing?
What is the primary purpose of a database management system (DBMS)?
option_a: To provide a user interface for the database
option_b: To store data in the database
option_c: To manage and manipulate data in the database
option_d: To provide security for the database

question: What is the primary function of a switch in a network?
option_c: To forward data packets to the appropriate device
option_d: To convert data from analog to digital

question: Which of the following is not a type of malware?
What is the primary purpose of normalization in a database?
option_a: To reduce data redundancy and improve data integrity
option_b: To increase data redundancy and improve data security
option_c: To provide a user interface for the database
option_d: To store data in the database

question: Which of the following is not a type of cloud computing?
What is the primary purpose of a compiler in programming?
option_a: To convert high-level language into machine language
option_b: To find errors in the program
option_c: To provide a user interface for the program

question: What is the primary function of a router in a network?
option_d: To forward data packets to the appropriate device

question: Which of the following is not a type of virtualization?
What is the primary purpose of a load balancer in a network?
option_c: To distribute network traffic evenly across multiple servers
option_d: To convert data from analog to digital

question: What is the primary purpose of a cache in computing?
option_a: To provide a backup for the data
option_c: To provide security for the data
option_d: To provide temporary storage for frequently used data

question: Which of the following is not a type of search algorithm?
Which of the following is not a principle of object-oriented programming?
Which of the following is not a type of network?
In the context of databases what does ACID stand for?
What is the main difference between a stack and a queue?
option_a: Stacks are static & queues are dynamic data structures
option_b: Stacks follow LIFO order & queues follow FIFO order
option_c: Stacks can only store integers & queues can store any data type
option_d: There is no difference & they are the same thing

question: What is the purpose of the virtual keyword in C++?
option_a: To create a pure virtual function
option_d: To create a virtual base class

question: What is the main purpose of a Trie data structure?
In the context of computer networks what does TCP stand for?
What is the purpose of the finally block in Java exception handling?
option_a: To define an alternative execution path
option_b: To execute code regardless of whether an exception is thrown or not
option_c: To handle a specific type of exception

question: What is the key difference between public and private inheritance in C++?
option_a: Public inheritance preserves base class member access but private does not
option_b: Private inheritance is more memory efficient than public inheritance
option_c: There is no difference they are the same thing
option_d: Private inheritance allows accessing private members but public does not

question: In the context of machine learning what is overfitting?
option_a: When a model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
option_b: When a model fails to capture underlying patterns in data
option_c: When a model is too simple to learn from data effectively
option_d: When a model has high bias and high variance

question: What is the key difference between GPU and CPU?
option_a: GPUs are better for parallel processing & CPUs are better for serial processing
option_b: GPUs consume more power than CPUs
option_c: GPUs are faster than CPUs for all types of computations
option_d: There is no difference they are interchangeable

question: What is the purpose of the volatile keyword in C/C++?
option_b: To prevent compiler optimizations that reorder operations
option_d: To indicate that a value may change unexpectedly

question: In the context of operating systems what is thrashing?
option_a: Excessive swapping of processes between main memory and disk
option_b: A type of memory leak caused by frequent allocation and deallocation
option_c: A condition where the CPU is constantly busy but no processes are making progress
option_d: A security vulnerability that allows unauthorized access to system resources

question: What is the purpose of the static keyword in Java?
option_c: To allocate memory for a variable at compile-time

question: In the context of databases what is denormalization?
option_a: The process of optimizing the database for better read performance
option_b: The process of removing redundant data from a database
option_c: The process of converting data into a more readable format
option_d: The process of creating additional indexes for faster queries

question: What is the purpose of the final keyword in Java?
option_b: To prevent a class from being subclassed

question: In the context of computer networks what is a NAT (Network Address Translation)?
option_a: A protocol for translating private IP addresses to public IP addresses
option_b: A type of firewall used to protect networks from unauthorized access
option_c: A technique for load balancing network traffic across multiple servers
option_d: A method for compressing network data to reduce bandwidth usage

question: What is the purpose of the friend keyword in C++?
option_a: To declare a function or class as a friend of another class
option_b: To create a friendship between two objects
option_c: To overload operators for a class
option_d: To optimize a function for better performance

question: In the context of machine learning what is a convolutional neural network (CNN)?
option_a: A type of neural network used for image classification and recognition
option_b: A neural network designed for time series forecasting
option_c: A type of unsupervised learning algorithm for clustering

question: What is the key difference between depth-first search (DFS) and breadth-first search (BFS)?
option_a: DFS explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking & BFS explores all nodes at the current depth before moving to the next level
option_b: DFS is more memory-efficient than BFS
option_c: BFS is guaranteed to find the shortest path & DFS is not

question: In the context of computer architecture what is pipelining?
option_a: A technique to improve CPU performance by breaking down instruction execution into a series of smaller steps
option_b: A way to connect multiple CPUs to increase processing power
option_c: A method for compressing data to improve storage efficiency
option_d: A parallel processing technique used in GPUs

question: In the context of databases what is an index?
option_a: A data structure used to optimize search and retrieval operations
option_b: A type of database query used for data manipulation
option_c: A technique for securing sensitive data in a database
option_d: A method for compressing data in a database to save storage space
